zh.Westminster, London, England: 英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Barbara Walter於1598年出生在英國薩默塞特時，她的父親Walter29歲，母親Dorothy Payne22歲。她於1619年1月17日與Gilbert Richards在英國戴文阿爾分頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世在英國。

zh-hans.Westminster, London, England: 英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Barbara Walter于1598年出生在英国薩默塞特时，她的父亲Walter29岁，母亲Dorothy Payne22岁。她于1619年1月17日与Gilbert Richards在英国戴文阿爾分頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Walthers
meanings: German: patronymic from the personal name Walther (see Walter ).

variants.name: McQuate
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uait ‘son of Uat’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Irish form McQuaid .

variants.name: Waltke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Jurgen, Wilhelmina.
German: from a pet form of any of the ancient Germanic personal names formed with Old High German waltan ‘to rule’ as the first element, as in Walter .

variants.name: McWaters
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaitéir, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of the personal name Walter , with excrescent English -s.

variants.name: Wahler
meanings: German:
from the personal name Wol(d)er, Low German form of Walter .
habitational name for someone from any of various places called Wahl, Wahle, or Wahlen.

variants.name: McWalter
meanings: Irish and Scottish: from Gaelic Mac Uaitéir ‘son of Walter ’.

variants.name: Walters
meanings: English: variant of Walter , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
German: patronymic from   Walter  .

variants.name: McQuaid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Colum, Nuala.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaid ‘son of Uad’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Scottish form McQuate .

variants.name: Walther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Erwin, Wolfgang, Otto, Gerhard, Gunter, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Bernd, Bernhard.
German, Dutch, Swedish, and Danish: variant of   Walter  .

variants.name: Lauter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Erwin, Kurt, Sigismund, Wolf.

German: topographic name and habitational name, from a common stream name meaning ‘clear’, from Middle High German hlutra, or from any of the places called Lauter or Lauta (Saxony). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and some other European countries.
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Lavtar or its Croatized and rare Slovenian variant Lautar: from a dialect variant of the personal name Valter (see Walter ).

nameMeanings.name: Barbara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

variants.name: Barbra
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Barbara Walter was born in 1598, in Somerset, England, her father, Walter, was 29 and her mother, Dorothy Payne, was 22. She married Gilbert Richards on 17 January 1619, in Alphington, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Barbara Walter est née en 1598, à Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Walter, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Payne, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Gilbert Richards le 17 janvier 1619, à Alphington, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Barbara Walter 1598 in Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter 29 und ihre Mutter Dorothy Payne 22. Sie hat am 17. Januar 1619 in Alphington, Devon, England Gilbert Richards geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Barbara Walter è nata il nel 1598 nel Somerset, England, sua padre, Walter, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Dorothy Payne, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Gilbert Richards il 17 gennaio 1619, nel Alphington, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel England.

ja.lifeSummary: Barbara Walterが1598年にイギリスソメルセトで生まれた時，父Walterは29歳，母Dorothy Payneは22歳であった。1619年1月17日にイギリスデボンアルフィントンでGilbert Richardsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Barbara Walter(이)가 1598년 영국 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Payne(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1619년 1월 17일 영국 데폰 알필그턴에서 Gilbert Richards(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Barbara Walter nasceu em 1598, em Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Payne, tinha 22 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Barbara Walter родилась в 1598 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Payne, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gilbert Richards 17 января 1619 г. в местности алфингтон, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Barbara Walter nació en 1598, en Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Dorothy Payne, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Gilbert Richards el 17 de enero de 1619, en Alphington, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en Inglaterra.
